note: The verb ending usually shows it's you, so "yo" is often dropped: "hablo" already means "I speak".
For a friend or someone your age. You already say "y tu?" = and you?
Same spelling as "el" (the), but here it means "he".
El and ella use the same verb form: el habla / ella habla.
From hablar. "I speak" = hablo (-o); he/she = habla (-a).
From trabajar. "I work" = trabajo; he/she = trabaja.
From vivir (an -ir verb). "I live" = vivo; he/she = vive (-e, not -a).
The "tu" form ends in -s: tu hablas = you speak.
Same -s ending: tu trabajas = you work.
